mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly UNRATE change shows no predictive power for 5-day equity moves (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized withdrawal of demand-side liquidity, which forces equity risk premiums higher and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above trend, equity markets price in higher corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days only when inflation remains anchored below 3.0 percent.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns when industrial output expands and inflation stays below 3.0%, or the conditional forward return differential between anchored and unanchored regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% consistently coincides with negative 5-day equity returns, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, the cost of capital suppresses equity valuations and reduces leverage capacity for corporate buybacks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive forward returns in more than 55% of trading sessions when the funds rate trades above 5.0%, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over a simple level-based monetary indicator.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls expand while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates a labor force participation surge that dilutes wage pressure, leading to lower inflation expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon, or the joint behavior of payroll growth and unemployment changes shows no statistically significant relationship with forward market returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn regime characterized by declining housing starts, equity markets anticipate a drag on construction-related earnings and consumer wealth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days only when industrial production remains flat.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns when housing starts fall for three months while industrial output holds steady, or the conditional forward return differential between flat and expanding industrial production regimes is not statistically significant.
